question_text: Which statement correctly describes the arrangement and movement of particles in a gas compared to a liquid?

options.label: A
options.text: Gas particles are more tightly packed than liquid particles and move slowly in fixed positions.

options.label: B
options.text: Gas particles are spread far apart with weak forces between them, so they move rapidly in all directions.

options.label: C
options.text: Liquid particles have no forces between them and move at the same speed as gas particles.

options.label: D
options.text: Gas and liquid particles have identical arrangements but different temperatures.

explanation: Gas particles are far apart with minimal attractive forces, allowing them to move freely and rapidly throughout their container. Liquid particles are closer together with stronger intermolecular forces, restricting their movement compared to gases.
